Dear Ms. Stone:
Last week you asked for additional information regarding Council
Member Steve McKee. SpecificaIIy, certain details regarding his
contractual relationship with John verner.
Please consider the following:
The agreement r^/as entered into in 1988. Upon execution of the
agreement, Steve McKee and his father were paid the sum of
$40,000. In accordance with the terms and conditions of the
agreement, in March of 1990, Steve McKee and his father were paid
an additional $25,000. The option was to expire in 1995 or 1996.
That specific information is available int he document of which
you have a copy.
Last year the option agreement was terminated by John Verner.
For your reference, I have enclosed a copy of that document.
I hope the foregoing wiII be of assistance to you.
Very truly yours,

RE: Request for Formal Written Advice/Government Code Section
83114 (b)
Gentlemen;
I have been asked to submit, on behaLf of Mayor Stephen McKee, a
request for formal written advice, in accordance with the
provisions of Government Code Section 83114(b). PIease be advised
that for purposes of this request for formal written advice, our
office is the authorized representative. Mayor McKee's address,
insofar as his official office is concerned, is 16775 Howland Road,
Lathrop, California. 
Please consider the following:
Mayor McKee and his father, Robert E. McKee, are the owners of a
ranch located near the corporate .limits of the City of Lathrop.
T he mayor and his father have entered into an agreement with a
Stockton development firm. 
For your reference, I have enclosed a
copy of that document, In accordance with the terms and conditions
of that agreement and addendum thereto, the Mayor and his father
were paid the sum of $25,000.00. The Stockton development firm,
Verner Construction, has sought to develop property in the City of
Lathrop. The Mayor abstained from participating in that decision.
The Mayor has since decided not to accept any further money from
Verner Construction for the option to purchase. The question with
which we are concerned is whether his decision, accompanied by an
appropriate modification of the agreement, would remove any
conflict of interest which would preclude him from participating in
any decision which would have a material financial effect on Verner
Construction. This is assuming that twelve months had elapsed


Fair PoIitical Practi-ces Commission
August 9, 1990
-Page f\"ro -
since his receipt of the payment of the $25,000.00 had occurred.
For your reference, I have also encLosed two opinions which I
prepared for Mayor McKee regarding this topic.
Should you have any further questions, please contact me. I have
been advised by Mayor McKee that he is more than wiLLing to travel
to your office in Sacramento, to meet with a representative of the
Commission, should clarifications or other information be needed.
Very truly yours,
Law Offices of
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER
CARL WAGGONER
Enclosures

Mayor Stephen McKee
John Bingham, City Manager

RE3 Letter No. 90-529/Request for Advice
Dear Mr. HaI labrin:
As you are aware, your office is in the process of preparing an
opinion on certain questions which I have asked on behalf of Mayor
Steven R. McKee.
Mayor McKee has requested that I forward to you a statement and
attachments which he prepared in opposition to a document prepared
by Beverly Kennedy, a City of Lathrop resident. Enclosed for your
review are those documents.
Mayor McKee believes that these
you in preparing your opinion,

atmosphere in the City
conflict of interest.
documents may be of assistance to
because they shed light on the
of Lathrop relative to his alleged
Should you have any questions concerning the foregoing, please

very truly yours,

FAX (2O9) 576 A437
cc: Mayor Steven McKee


lRO TM OESK OF
STEIf,Ef R- tcGE
Page One of Three
July 25, 1990
For one year now, I have been silent. 
.1 istening to tlte repeated accusations of
wrongdolng fron Beverl.y Kennedy concerning mv actions as Mayor of the Citv of
Lathrop. No longer will I remain silent. i feel I must respond. I owe it to
this Conmunity to prove to you the ailegations made are false and I can
substantlate ny clains with evidence.
Referrlng to Beverly Kennedy's "Connents foI Lathrop General Plan & Related
Developnent Regulatlons" dated July 23, 1990, which she subnitted for the record
to the Cltlzens Advisory Com,nittee for the General PIan, accusations have once
again been levied against me and I submit the following to refute her
allegatlons once and for all.
Reference Paragraph 1 rel.ative to the assertion that I was willing to /give
Manteca the Kearny Ventures Project: I submit Exhibits A. B and C provj.ng to
the contrary.
Reference Paragraph 1 relative to the asscrtion that I attended a meeting with
Kearny Ventures and Manteca Officials prior to lncorporation, I submit that I
was invited to the meeting, as a .landowner west of I-5, and elected to attend in
the spirlt of cooperation as I have always c.laimed to be willing to ]isten to
anyone at anytime. Up until the tine I arrived at the meeting, I was not aware
that Manteca 0fficials would even be present or participating in the meetins.
It is true the Incorporation Committee was not aware of this meeting, however. I
was attending as a landowner and interested party and was Dot j.n attendance on
behalf of the Incorporation Committee or ar)vone else for that nattgr.
Reference Paragraph 1 relatlve to the assertion that I clained my reasons for
running for office was "none of her business". I subnit Exhibit D, a letter
from Mr. Jose Perez who ,ras witness to the conversation between nyself and Mrs.
Kennedy. AddltionalIy, I nade it perfectly clear that I was a farmer, that I
farmed 150 acres in Lathrop in my campaiqn documents. I never once denied a
conflict and advised alL Council Members and City Stal'i of my potential confl.ict
and deferred to City Staff for interpretation of Conflict Laws and
recommendatlon for those matters from whic:: I rus'L abstain,
Addltlonally, in a phone conversation with Mrs. Georgianna Reichelt, (the "other
person" alluded to) who was also campaiqn nanage. for another City Council
Candidate, I advlsed Mrs. Reichelt that in the event the issue was raised about
my landholdings during the campaign, I certain.lv wDuld address the issue as 1
felt lt was no secret to the Communitv. My wife was a witness to mv end of this
phone conversation.
l

Re: opinion,/Confl-ict of Interest
Dear Mayor McKee:
You have asked me for an opinion regarding the issue of whether
you should participate in any decision regarding John Verner, an
indivi,dual who is developing a subdivision wilhin the ccrporate
Iimits of the City of Lathrop.
You have informed me thac you have a business relationship with
Mr. verner from the standgoint that he has entered into a sales
contract with you and your father for Lhe purchase of real pro-
perty located outside of the City Iimits of Lathrop.
Consummation of the sale is dependent upon the property being
annexed to the City. 
You and your father, io accordance wilh
the terms and conditi.ons of the agreement, have recently received
tne sum of $25,000 as and for the extension of the agreemenE.
It is my conclusion that you should that you shoul-d not par-
ticipate in any decision which will have a material financial
ef f ect on I,1r. Verner.
Government Cooe Section 87i-00 prohibits, generaily, a public
official 
from participating in any governmental decisj.on in which
he has a iinancial interest. 
Section 87100 provides:
pubtic officiaL at any l-evel of state or local
government shall make/ participate in making in any way
atlempc t.o use hj.s official position to influence a
governmental decision in whicn he knows or has reason to
k:row he has a f i.nancial interest. "
To determine whether a financial interest exists, it is necessary
tc reier to Govelnment Code Section 87103. Section 87103 provides
in the perlinenE Part:


1"1r. Steven McKee, Mayor
City of La th rop
March 14, 1990
Page 2
" 
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public aenerally, on the official or a member or her
immediate family or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial J-ending institutlon 
in
the reguLar course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official stat.us, aggregating
two hundred fifty 
dolJ.ars (5250) or more in value pro-
vided to, received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made.
Preliminari.Ly, if it is reasonably foreseeable that participation
in any decision on your part viould cause a material financial
effect on the real property you own, then you should abstain from
such action. Further, as appears in Section 87103 you should not
participate if participation would have a material financial
effect on any source of income.
The question which arises is whether or not. John Verner is a
source of income to you. Government Code Section 82030 defines
income, a copy of which is enclosed for your reference.
As appears in Sections 82030, income includes "proceeds from any
sale". Although strictly 
speaking, the proceeds which you
received were no! from the sale of land, it is proceeds from the
sale of a right tso purchase. Therefore, l4r. verner is a source
of income to you.
t hope the foregoj.ng answered your inqL:iry. Should you have any
further questions, please contact me.
very truly yours,
Law Offices of
KROLL, LOEFFLER & 
'{AGGONER
Carl O. waggoner
CW : lms

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor McKee can participate in governmental decisions affecting John Verner, a developer with whom McKee has a business relationship involving property sales and option agreements that have generated $25,000-$40,000 in payments to McKee and his father.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Multiple documents bundled together - primarily incoming requests and correspondence rather than FPPC response letters. Document contains attorney opinions, informal advice requests dated January 1994 and August 1990, option agreement termination letter from June 1993, and Mayor's statement from July 1990. No formal FPPC advice letter/conclusion found in this packet. Parsing confidence reduced due to multiple overlapping documents with different dates and purposes.; Date cleared: parsed 1994-01-73 but file year is 1990

embedding.summary: This document package contains correspondence and supporting materials related to FPPC File No. 90-529, concerning potential conflicts of interest for Mayor Stephen McKee of Lathrop regarding his business dealings with developer John Verner. The materials include informal advice requests, formal advice requests, attorney opinions on conflict of interest, termination of the option agreement, and Mayor McKee's response to allegations by a resident.
